front: Work: the mestizo Crisostomo Ibarra returns and comes into conflict with the corrupt friar Padre Damaso, who is the real father of Maria Clara
Work: a 'yellowish individual' is crushed by the stone he drops from a crane at the dedication of a school building
Work: the dedication vows to treat the country's 'social cancer' using the ancient method of placing the sick on temple steps
Work: Sisa goes insane and dies after her sons Crispin and Basilio are accused of stealing from a convent
Work: Ibarra returns disguised as the jeweler Simoun (wearing blue goggles) and plans to bomb a wedding with a pomegranate-shaped lamp
Work: Simoun laces a house with explosives and leaves a warning reading 'Mene, Thecel, Pares' (alluding to Belshazzar's feast)
Work: written the night before this author's execution; smuggled out in an alcohol stove; proclaims 'to die is to rest'
Work: the untitled poem was later given its name by friends; read to the U.S. Congress by Henry Cooper
Work: inscribed on a bronze monument designed by Richard Kissling titled Motto Stella; translated by Charles Derbyshire
Author: Ferdinand Blumentritt is exclusively famous for his letters to this Filipino national hero
Author: Benedict Anderson invoked this author's idea of the 'devil of comparisons'
Author: Unamuno compared this author to a 'Tagalog Hamlet'
Author: this author's character Maria Clara was inspired by his lover Leonor Rivera
Author: also an ophthalmologist; conversant in 22 languages; a namesake law mandates Philippine schools teach about him
Author: Ambeth Ocampo discovered this author's unfinished novel Makamisa
Author: credited with popularizing the folktale 'The Monkey and the Turtle'
